name: Carrot Walnut Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Lightly grease and flour a 10-by-4-inch tube pan.
3. Sift together the flour, baking powder, baking soda, cinnamon, and salt; set aside.
4. Wash and peel carrots; grate on medium grater or use the coarse blade of a food processor. Grated carrots should measure about 4 cups.
5. In a large bowl, beat butter and both sugars with an electric mixer until light and fluffy, about 4 minutes. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition until smooth and light.
6. Combine lemon juice, orange juice, lemon peel, and orange peel in a measuring cup; add to the flour mixture in thirds, alternating with the lemon-orange mixture, beginning and ending with the flour mixture. Beat just until smooth, about 1 minute.
7. Stir in grated carrots, nuts, and raisins; mix well. Turn into prepared tube pan; spread evenly.
8. Bake for 60 minutes, or until a cake tester inserted in the center comes out clean. Cool in the pan on a wire rack for 20 minutes to cool slightly.
9. Meanwhile, make cream-cheese glaze: In a medium bowl, combine cream cheese, lemon juice, lemon peel, and confectioners' sugar. Beat with an electric mixer until smooth; set aside.
10. Loosely loosen the edge of the cake with a spatula.
11. Turn out of pan onto a rack. Spread glaze over the top of the warm cake, letting it run down the sides. Carefully remove the cake to a serving platter. Sprinkle chopped walnuts around the top edge of the cake. Serves 12.
